dialogos.texto_en: I didn't realize the Dream Center was a greenhouse.\nI couldn't imagine that from the name.
Besides, from the outside, it looks like a tower.\nWhat's the intention behind that?\nThat's way too mysterious, isn't it?
I heard it's not a tower, but a lookout platform...\nAnyway, the flowers were beautiful and worth our time,\nright?
True, and taking in so many different fragrances was fantastic.
And, besides that tower,\nthere were many buildings with really picturesque views.
The stained-glass hall was beautiful,\nbut the corridor with the baobab trees...
The waterfront with its blooming water lilies\nand Western-style gazebo.\nIt didn't feel like Japan.
No doubt.\nIt had an ancient ruins-like atmosphere.
Teehee!\nI took a lot of pictures.\nI should organize them a little.
Hm?
Hey, Nino, hold on a sec.
Huh? What is it?
Didn't I just see myself\non the screen of your smartphone just now?
What the—?!\nDon't just look at my smartphone without permission!
I wasn't looking at it, I just happened to see it.\nAnd hey, aren't you the one\ntaking pictures of people without their permission?
Well, the pictures turned out great, didn't they?\nI didn't take any pictures of a weird face\nor anything, so it's fine, right?
You're taking weird face shots too?
Whaddya think?
You are, aren't you?!\nYou're not that good at acting dumb!
So what?\nOnly I'll look at them,\nand I won't show them to anyone else.
No way, delete them!\nHand me your smartphone, I'll erase all the data!
I will absolutely NOT give you my smartphone.\nIt's a violation of privacy\nto look at someone else's phone.
Oh, my memory is almost full.\nWe've taken a lot of pictures\non this graduation trip alone.
Are you taking that many pictures of me?
Don't take pictures of people without their consent.
Why not?\nIt's okay if I don't show them to anyone else, right?
That's not the issue here!
I mean, try to understand\nwanting to keep pictures of your significant other.
Huh?
Well... I guess that's something I can understand\nto an extent.
So it's fine, isn't it?
But still, no more secret photos.
Oh, my memory is almost full.\nWe've taken a lot of pictures\non this graduation trip alone.
Are you taking that many pictures of me?
I'm gonna take pictures of you too!\nI'll take as many of you as you took of me!
I see. Take as many as you want,\nand look at them when you feel lonely.
Huh?!\nWell, I didn't really mean it like that, but...
So, when you feel lonely, Nino,\nyou'll look at my photos?
Yeah, I will. It'll remind me of the time\nI took them, and the conversations we had.
Thus, when you too feel lonely,\nlook at my photos and fill your heart with my love.
I may not be able to be by your side,\nbut my heart will always be with you, Futaro.
I understand.
Oh, my memory is almost full.\nWe've taken a lot of pictures\non this graduation trip alone.
Of me?
Aren't WE self-conscious.\nThe pictures aren't just about you. \nThey're about the scenery and stuff.
Oogh...
Including pictures of the others too.
Come to think of it,\nmaybe I started taking more photos of them\nafter we transferred schools.
Is that so?
I wonder why?\nI feel like we just didn't have\nmany opportunities before.
I think all five of us have changed...\nsince we transferred schools and met you, Futaro.
I mean it in a good way, of course.\nDon't worry.
I'm not worried about that.\nI've changed for the better too.
(Meeting the quintuplets, and Nino,\n has definitely made me a better person.)
I see. You too, huh, Futaro.
I'm really glad to have met you.\nTruly...
